{{Quote|This is new…|[[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]] to [[Saesee Tiin/Legends|Saesee Tiin]] at the [[Battle of Iktotch|Battle of]] [[Iktotch/Legends|Iktotch]]|<ref name="Heavy Metal Jedi">{{CWACite|1|Heavy Metal Jedi}}</ref>}}
The '''B3 ultra battle droid''' was a powerful prototype [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droid]] designed by the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] and deployed approximately five [[Galactic Standard Calendar|months]] after the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|Battle of]] [[Geonosis/Legends|Geonosis]].

==Characteristics==
[[File:B3_ultra_battle_droid_TotG.jpg|thumb|left|180px|A [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] confronted by a B3 ultra battle droid]]
The B3 ultra battle [[Droid/Legends|droid]] was based on the [[B2-series super battle droid/Legends|B2 super battle droid]] model, though it was far larger and bulkier than the B2, and boasted more substantial armor. The B3 had two large primary arms: the right arm, containing a tight-spray [[Flamethrower/Legends|flamethrower]]; and the left arm housing a wide-spray [[Plasma cannon/Legends|plasma cannon]].
Two smaller secondary arms, located underneath the primary limbs, featured deadly rapid-fire forearm [[Blaster cannon/Legends|blaster cannons]], similar to the B2 battle droid.<ref name="NEGD" />

Built into the droid's left shoulder was a missile launcher containing [[brilliant missile]]s, capable of highly sophisticated target acquisition and tracking. The design also featured a [[Density projector]], a device capable of increasing the droid's weight twenty-fold. This enabled it to lock itself in place and made it nearly impossible to topple over, counteracting the droid's high center of gravity, although doing so would make it an easier target for Republic heavy weapons.<ref name="NEGD" />

==History==
{{Quote|…but clearly, even your acrobatics weren't enough to stop that thing.|Saesee Tiin to Mace Windu at the Battle of Iktotch|<ref name="Heavy Metal Jedi" />}}
The B3 ultra battle droid was by far the deadliest single battle droid manufactured by the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]]. Thankfully for the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]], the droids suffered from technical problems and were never mass produced.

[[Wat Tambor/Legends|Wat Tambor]] had held a challenge for two teams, [[Metalorn/Legends|Metalorn]] and [[Foundry/Legends|Foundry]]. Foundry had developed a droid known as the Avatar-7. This was the droid which received [[General/Legends|General]] [[Grievous/Legends|Grievous]]' blessing. It was dubbed the B3 and was commissioned as a frontline battle droid. [[Baktoid Combat Automata/Legends|Baktoid Combat Automata]] never visualized having them sent into combat in large numbers like [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|B1-series battle droid]]s, owing to the B3's high cost per unit. B3 units were used by the Trade Federation for frontal assaults against well-defended targets. They were often used in place of [[Droideka/Legends|droidekas]], being far cheaper than the destroyer droids.

[[File:Ultra_battle_droid.jpg|thumb|200px|[[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]] and [[Saesee Tiin/Legends|Saesee Tiin]] face a B3 ultra battle droid.]]

[[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Masters]] [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]] and [[Saesee Tiin/Legends|Saesee Tiin]] faced the Avatar-7 prototype at the [[Battle of Iktotch|Battle of]] [[Iktotch/Legends|Iktotch]].<ref name="CSWE">''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref> Though their individual skills were not enough to overcome the behemoth, the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] worked together to defeat it, eventually destroying it by crushing it under the remains of a [[IG-227 Hailfire-class droid tank/Legends|''Hailfire'' droid]]. B3s were also used during the [[Outer Rim Sieges/Legends|Outer Rim Sieges]], where they served as sparse heavy units under individual Separatist commanders. However, exorbitant manufacturing costs and persistent malfunctions with the droid's density projectors prevented widespread use.

After the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], hundreds of units were used at the [[Imperial Academy/Legends|Imperial Academy's]] urban-warfare training zone on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Carida/Legends|Carida]].
